title: Vomit on Upholstery
timeEstimate: 20 minutes to several hours; escalate early on soak-through
stainChemistry: Vomit is a complex mix: partially digested food (protein, carbohydrate, fat), stomach acid (HCl, pH 1.5–3.5), bile salts (natural surfactant that drives stain deep), mucus, and proteins (pepsin, food proteins). The acid can damage dyes and weaken fibers if left in contact — quick neutralization matters. The protein fraction bonds and sets with heat (protein-before-heat rule). Bile salts act as emulsifiers and help the stain penetrate the batting. Odor comes from volatile fatty acids and bacterial breakdown of protein + food residue. On upholstery, the invariant failure mode is batting / foam saturation — vomit carries liquid down into the cushion where surface chemistry can't reach. Upholstery code tag (W / W-S / S / X) determines whether water-based cleaning is even allowed.
whyThisWorks: Solid removal + cold water flush + enzyme + acetic-acid neutralization + weighted extraction is the standard pro protocol for vomit on upholstery. Enzyme digests the protein matrix. Dawn lifts fat and bile. Dilute white vinegar or acetic acid neutralizes residual acid and reduces odor. Aggressive weighted extraction between rounds prevents wick-back. The ceiling: if vomit soaked into batting, home chemistry only clears the surface. Upholstery pro-escalation threshold is LOW — honest early escalation is better than grinding through four rounds of half-success.
defaultAssumption: Kid / pet / night-before-too-much context. Fresh + caught-in-the-act on a W/W-S code cushion = solid home try. Dried or older, bigger than a hand, on an S-only or X-code upholstery, or soaked-through = pro early. Don't over-wet upholstery trying to chase odor deeper than a home cloth can reach.

spottingProtocol.technique: Biohazard PPE, code tag read, spoon-scrape
spottingProtocol.equipment: Disposable gloves, mask optional, spoon or dull edge, garbage bag
spottingProtocol.instruction: Gloves on (mask for pet vomit or if immunocompromised in household). Find and read the upholstery care code (usually tag under a cushion): W or W-S = water OK, proceed. S-only = solvent-only, NOT for vomit, call a pro. X = vacuum only, pro. Remove solids with a spoon or dull edge — scrape, don't rub — into a sealed garbage bag. Don't work liquid deeper into the cushion.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cotton towel, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cold water. Press on the spot, lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. Transfers vomit into the cloth while diluting the stomach acid before it damages dye. Never pour. Weighted dry towel 5 min to extract surface moisture.

spottingProtocol.technique: Damp-cloth enzyme for protein + food matrix
spottingProtocol.equipment: Liquid Protein Spotter OR enzyme pretreat (Nature's Miracle, Biz, OxiClean Versatile per label) in warm water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Warm water (NOT hot — under 100°F / 38°C — warm enough for enzyme activity, not hot enough to set protein). Enzyme solution on damp cloth, press on the spot, dwell 15–20 min. Digests protein and food residue.
Dawn in cool water on a damp cloth. Work in with a soft toothbrush. Lifts fat and bile. Damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Equal parts white vinegar + cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dilute white vinegar on damp cloth. Press, dwell 5 min. Neutralizes residual acid and breaks down volatile fatty acids that cause lingering odor. Blot dry.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Heavy dry towel + weight, baking soda
spottingProtocol.instruction: Weighted dry towel 30 min to wick moisture back out of batting. Dust baking soda generously, leave overnight with a fan pointed at the area. Vacuum off next morning. Weighted extraction matters more than fancy chemistry on upholstery.
Fan the area fully dry. Smell check in daylight the next day. Any lingering odor or wick-back ring = batting is contaminated, home ceiling reached, pro low-moisture extraction.

steps: 1. Gloves + mask. Code tag check. S/X = pro. Scrape solids.
6. Weighted dry towel 30 min + baking soda overnight + fan.
7. Odor / wick-back return = pro low-moisture extraction.
products: Liquid Protein Spotter / enzyme pretreat (Nature's Miracle / Biz / OxiClean Versatile)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool to warm (<100°F/38°C) — never hot, never steam
warnings: CODE CHECK FIRST — S-only / X are pro lanes, not DIY
NEVER hot water or steam — sets protein, spreads acid damage
NEVER pour liquid on upholstery — batting saturation is the failure mode
Low pro-escalation threshold — honest early escalation beats 4 half-rounds
Never ammonia cleaners on vomit — pets / kids smell them as urine

homeSolutions.instruction: Fresh vomit caught within minutes on W or W-S upholstery = doable at home. Dried, older, bigger than a hand, on an S-only code, through to the batting, or causing ongoing odor in the room after treatment = call a pro. Upholstery is where over-treating makes things worse — you can't rinse it the way you can rinse laundry. If the cushion cover zips off and is machine-washable, that is by far the best option for fresh vomit.

Gloves on — this is biohazard. Mask if it's pet vomit or if anyone in the house is immunocompromised. Find the upholstery code (usually on a tag under a cushion): W or W-S = water-based cleaning OK, proceed. S-only = solvent-only, NOT for vomit, pro call. X = vacuum only, pro. If the cushion cover zips off and is W-marked, take it off and machine-wash it cold (remove solids first, cold cycle, mild detergent + white vinegar rinse) — that's vastly easier than treating in place.

Use a spoon or the edge of a plastic card to scoop solids into a garbage bag. Scrape, don't rub or wipe — rubbing drives the liquid deeper into the cushion and the batting where chemistry can't reach. Seal the garbage bag.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Blot — Dilute the Acid
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a clean white towel with COLD water and press firmly on the spot. Lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. Cold water dilutes the stomach acid before it damages the dye. Never pour — over-wetting carries vomit into the batting. Keep pressing until the towel stops picking up color.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat on a Damp Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: In WARM (not hot — bath temperature, under 100°F) water, dissolve an enzyme pretreat — OxiClean Versatile, Biz, or a pet-stain enzyme cleaner like Nature's Miracle. Dampen a white cloth in the enzyme solution, press on the spot, cover with a damp towel for 15–20 minutes. The enzyme digests the protein and food matrix. Hot water would set the protein — keep it warm.
Work a drop of Dawn in cool water into the spot with a soft toothbrush. Dawn emulsifies the fat and bile salts in vomit that the enzyme didn't reach. Blot with a damp cloth — don't pour water.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar + Water Rinse — Neutralize Acid + Odor
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix equal parts white vinegar + cool water. Dab on a damp cloth, press on the spot 5 minutes. This neutralizes residual stomach acid and breaks down the volatile compounds that cause the lingering smell. This is the step most home guides skip and it's what separates "stain gone, smell lingers" from "fully clean."

Press a thick DRY towel with a heavy book on the spot for 30 minutes — pulls moisture back up out of the batting. Then dust baking soda generously over the area. Leave overnight with a fan pointed at it. Vacuum off in the morning. Baking soda + airflow + time = dry batting = no returning smell.

Nose to the cushion next morning in daylight. Any lingering smell or a wick-back ring = the batting is still contaminated, home chemistry is at its ceiling. Tell a pro: "Vomit on [W / W-S / code unknown] upholstery, age [fresh / overnight], home-treated with enzyme + Dawn + vinegar + baking soda + fan-dry. [Odor persists / wick-back visible / batting suspect]. Please use low-moisture surface extraction — do not saturate."

steps: 1. Honest scope — code S/X / soak-through = pro. Zip-off cover = wash cover.
2. Gloves + mask. Code-tag check (W / W-S / S / X).
3. Scrape solids into sealed bag — never rub.
4. Cold damp-cloth blot to dilute acid + wick vomit.
5. Enzyme pretreat in WARM (not hot) water, damp cloth, 15–20 min.
6. Dawn + soft toothbrush to lift fat + bile.
7. Dilute white vinegar damp cloth, 5 min — neutralizes acid + odor.
8. Weighted dry towel 30 min + baking soda overnight + fan.
9. Smell check next day. Persistent = pro low-moisture extraction.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile / Biz / Nature's Miracle)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if upholstery code is S-only or X, if vomit soaked through into batting, if area is larger than a hand, if the cushion cover doesn't zip off and the spill is big, or if odor persists after a full home cycle.
warnings: CODE CHECK — S/X are NOT DIY lanes for vomit
NEVER hot water — sets protein + spreads acid damage
NEVER steam-clean vomit — steam sets protein
NEVER ammonia-based cleaners — pets/kids re-mark smell
Batting saturation = pro-only — home ceiling is surface

neverDo: Never hot water or steam on vomit
Never ammonia cleaners — invites re-marking by pets, smells like urine to kids
Never skip the code tag check
fiberSensitivities: W / W-S code: tolerates full protocol
S-only: solvent-only cleaning, NOT for water-based stain — pro
Leather upholstery: different protocol entirely — use leather-specific card
Microfiber / suede-like: test in hidden area first

materialWarnings: Batting saturation = pro; home is surface-only
Vinegar + warm water rinse is the odor step most people skip

escalation.when: Upholstery code is S or X, leather, wool, spill larger than a hand, batting soak suspected, cushion cover isn't removable and spill was large, or odor persists after a full home cycle.
escalation.whatToTell: "Vomit on upholstery — code [W / W-S / S / X / unknown], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with enzyme + Dawn + white vinegar + baking soda + fan-dry. [Odor persists / wick-back ring / batting saturation suspected]. Please use low-moisture surface extraction — do not saturate."
escalation.specialistType: professional upholstery cleaner with low-moisture / hot-water extraction (not truck-mount saturation)

contextQuestions: What's the upholstery code — W, W-S, S, or X? (under cushion tag)
Does the cushion cover zip off and is it machine-washable?
Did it soak into the cushion or stay on the surface?
Any ammonia cleaner or hot water used already?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Zip-off washable cushion covers change everything — wash the cover cold with enzyme detergent + vinegar rinse, that's far more effective than in-place treatment. Pet vomit often has bile that stains dye — prompt acid-neutralization matters. Cushion batting is the ceiling for home chemistry; if batting is saturated, pro low-moisture extraction is the right tool, NOT truck-mount saturation.
